author: Neto Moreno (Performance Coach)
content:
**RULES RULES RULES FOR FRIDAY**

Seriously, take a screenshot of this and keep it handy. 

**If you’re green for the week**, only trade with a small **PORTION of your profits**. For example, if you’re up $1,000, risk just $200-$300. Even if you lose it, you’ll still end the week green. 

If you’re green but don’t have enough profits to play with, **DO NOT TRADE.**

**If you’re red for the week, DO NOT TRADE.**

Even if you’re green and have profits to spare, you can always take Friday off. Enjoy a 3-day weekend—it’s never a bad idea. 

Remember, **Fridays won’t make you, but they can break you**. 

Stick to these rules, and you’ll never end the week on a bad note.

@everyone <@&1219020123218645102>   

Please confirm you have read this with a 🤑

author: chunky_beast
content:
<@584423632802021376> I’ll keep this as simple as possible, but please what I’m about to say isn’t meant to be gospel. I don’t like leaving questions unanswered if I can help it. 

When you look at the upcoming earnings you obviously expect an uptick in options trading into that particular week volume wise but what will almost always increase is options holders/open interest (OI) into those options. Using AAPL for example we have a large disparity between Call holders vs Put holders for Nov 1 Weekly’s so with this we can estimate that, as of Friday, the market is currently expecting bullish move into earnings. Plus as you get from Earnings Whispers, on the wider options there was a large volume of Feb 21 265C bought on Friday indicating some bull expects a move towards the upside in the longer term worth putting up $12M. With all this you can infer the market a bullish outlook on AAPL. 

A different case is GOOGL where we can see, as of Friday, there isn’t really any high expectations for explosive short term moves based on next weeks options OI. 

There are other equities you can look at with extremely high bullish option sentiment like AMZN and AMD. 

However this is just a gauge, not a crystal ball. Do your own research and ensure that this correlates with your strategy and playbook. Also if you’re gonna trade shorter dated options into earnings this isn’t a swing where you may lose 60-70%. You can easily lose your equity so you have to be comfortable with a 100% risk to capital overnight.

author: Jdub
content:
Its not really about being upset because as a human you are going to have emotions. It's all about what you do with those emotions. Am I upset when I take a bigger loss than expected. Of course but its all about how you respond and what you do after the fact. 

Trading is all a game about probabilities. I had conviction coming into this morning on TSLA for a continuation higher and guess what it didn't work out. I wanted to only risk around $2000 on this trade and ended up losing over $4000. But what happened next, I didn't do anything irrational or went to do something dumb. What I did was wait for another opportunity to setup. 

We ended up getting in SPY/ES which was a solid setup and is still working out as I type this out. 

As for relying on trading as income I didn't do this for the longest time. When I started out and even when I was profitable I was still delivering packages on the side. I would next recommend someone to do this full time without some sort of income coming in. Having to "rely" on trading is going to make it very difficult no matter who you are. 

Hope that makes some more sense.

author: Kam
content: PLTR in new high territory so it could def keep running, but generally i look for at least 3-5 days momo in a new trend for swing which on weekly chart started this week with earnings. Short term is stretched above daily 9EMA so you would need a wider stop perhaps down to that gap-low at 47 if starting a new long position up here so not exactly good R/R. i definitely wouldn't try and anticipate a short on something this strong is my best advice 🙂 if want to look for a top you want to see more of a parabolic type move with blow-off volume and bearish price action on daily (reverse hammer)

author: TonyMontana
content:
What’s up guys!

Hope everyone is having a good weekend after a BIG week last week.

We have crypto pumping today and markets overall bullish.

A quick message to be cautious this week,

Monday we have a bank holiday but markets are open expect low volume and chop.

After that overall on most charts we do need a pullback.

I know fomo is high for most people right now but don’t do anything dumb this week (full size, low quality trades, impulsive trades)

Let’s keep following our rules and look for high quality setups this week.

Remember it’s about staying in the game long term.

A lot of people see the $130k trade in a week and think it took a week.

No.

It took 7+ years of learning and long nights.

With this being said, I’ll catch you on voice Monday at 9:25AM EST!

@everyone

author: Lauren (lakatrades)
content:
Thank you so much for your kind words and for recognizing the progress I’ve made it truly means a lot. The turning point for me was realizing that trading is all about psychological thinking. I had to learn to trust my knowledge and analysis, stay focused, and, most importantly, put emotions aside. Scaling up became much easier once I disciplined myself to stick to my trading plan, even though the setups stayed the same.
I’ve failed many times in the past because I didn’t stick to my plan and let emotions take over. It wasn’t until I committed to following the plan and staying disciplined that things started to change. Your mention of the Plateau of Latent Potentialreally hit home it’s such a powerful concept. There were so many moments when I felt like all my hard work wasn’t paying off, but eventually, everything started to click. Breaking through that phase took persistence and mindset shifts, and it felt amazing.
And no, I wasn’t upset at all by your gesture it actually made me happy! It’s great to know someone else understands and celebrates these milestones with me. Thank you so much for your support and encouragement it truly means a lot. 

Trust the process:)

author: Neto Moreno (Performance Coach)
content:
Hi Team,

**__Post-Holiday Trading Rules—Keep it Simple!__**

Today’s a short day after Thanksgiving, so expect **low volume** and **aggressive time decay**. It’s the perfect day to follow Mentor <@717554586843611217>’s advice: **watch the market only**. Turn off your brokerage, keep your charts on, and observe.

Remember, patience and discipline are key—there’s no need to force trades on days like this. Protect your capital and enjoy the rest of the holiday weekend!

Cheers!

@everyone <@&1219020123218645102>

author: Pierogi
content: I had one bad trade that almost wiped me out but to avoid that in the future I learned and now only risking max 10% of my account size. It sucks  when I see you guys making thousands of dollars in profit and I only makink a couple of hundreds but I believe that is the way to survive longterm..

author: DWC2016
content:
Yea makes sense 100%. But risk and position size are not the same thing and often confused. 

Like Tony will have a position size of $30k but only risk $4k if the trade goes bad which would be %4 risk if the account if it’s a $100k account but if the trade works that how you get those $50k+ days while only risk a small amount. 

Or Ex : You can put on a trade that’s $1000 in size but only actually risk $100-$200 as the stop loss. I think a lot of people get confused between the position size and the stop loss which is the actual risk in the trade. Hope that makes sense. I was confused by this when starting as well 🙏

author: Mk
content:
My absolute biggest blunder of a problem is that I do not wait for confirmation. My trade ideas are correct and have learned much from Tony and Jack. But a large part in my trading stems from having the lowest risk possible so I end up trying to catch the bottom for long positions without waiting for confirmation.  Today I had a $1,400 loss on TSLA because I stopped out too early. I re took it later and made $1,600 on it.  Green but could of avoided unnecessary draw down.

My question is, what type of confirmations do you guys look for on trending days? Green 5 min candle close above the level with strong volume or something else?  <@1014389514141184074> <@1121621751764369501> <@792532341565554728>

author: Neto Moreno (Performance Coach)
content:
For this setup to work best, there are some essential components:

Break of the level – Price needs to decisively break above or below the identified support or resistance.
Displacement – There should be a noticeable and strong move away from the level after the break. This ensures momentum and liquidity have shifted.
Retest – Price then retraces to retest the broken level.
Reaction – Finally, observe how price reacts during the retest. Strong rejection or confirmation signals the continuation of the move, offering a potential entry point.

Waiting for all these steps helps filter out false breakouts and increases the probability of a successful trade.

author: BrokeAintNoJoke
content:
I took the put trade yesterday because of multiple confluences. Price had retraced to the 61% fib level which correlated closely to the KL from previous PDH (I read trading bible over last weekend in books channel), PA was showing wicks up for a few bars showing sellers keeping price from breaching, there was a perfect test of the 1min ORB low and I think at the same time Qs and Spy showed weakness. 
I did not expect it to go down that quickly and exited all cons in the same fashion missing out on the huge move downward. I failed to leave trailers and scale. Also exited completely because just minutes prior I was in a call that at peak was 3x what I closed scaling 25% then 75% at breakeven. One lesson is I need to remember to leave trailers. Missed the big move downward. could have been a $3k day instead of $550 if I left trailers. That said I am happy that I am starting to see profitable entries.

author: StevenP
content: the 119 is 1 otm since stock price is 118. 1 otm strikes are a good balance for day traders to take. usually they have a good delta and the cost is decent. so it makes for good profit compared to cost. if you look at 2 otm sometimes the volume and open interest paired with your thesis on thinking the stock will go to $120 from 118 can be more profitable with a strong move towards the strike price. its really all about your risk and thesis on where the stock will go

author: Lauren (lakatrades)
content:
Copy of the email which I rec'd from Tony - 

If you’re reading this email, you've already experienced our Accelerator and may have some idea about Traders Lab.

However, I want to take a step back and give you a clear overview of what we’ve built and who this is for.

​

The mission of Traders Lab is to help traders optimize their performance to build a profitable career through tailored education.

Anyone that’s been able to become a profitable trader for a consistent period, it took them at least a few years to get everything right.

Our goal is to cut down those years of trials and tribulations.

​

We spent the last year building Traders Lab to ensure it perfectly supports every trader’s journey, regardless of their experience level.

Whether you’re starting out or are somewhere in that messy middle phase of your trading this is for you.

​

Here’s a breakdown of the three main pillars of Traders Lab:

Curriculum: Our playbooks are the most in-depth trading resources we’ve ever created.
​
Each playbook is meticulously designed with step-by-step modules, detailed examples, and proven best practices to ensure you’re learning the right way from day one.
​
Tailored: We understand that every trader has unique goals, experience levels, and learning styles.
​
That’s why our platform adapts to your specific needs, providing personalized learning paths and targeted resources.
​
Community: The ecosystem is at the heart of what makes Traders Lab special.
​
We’ve created an environment where both beginners and experienced traders can network, share insights, and grow together.

​

And that’s just the tip of the iceberg…

There’s so much more inside Traders Lab that I can’t fit into one email but will share more over the next couple of weeks.

​

Now, anyone can just say all of the above, but actually putting it to work is what drives us.

And, I want to show you just one example of what Traders Lab can do for you.

Here’s one of our top members Paul, who has been able to transform almost every aspect in his trading since joining Traders Lab.

Take a look at his recent message inside our community:


​

We've kept Traders Lab discrete and only available to a limited number of people.

We haven't let anyone from the public join us - that's how much we're prioritizing quality in this program.

Since you've experienced our Accelerator I wanted to extend you the opportunity to potentially join Traders Lab if it's a good fit.

All you have to do is a book a call with our team, if Traders Lab is a fit for your needs we'll offer you the opportunity to join us.

​Click here to book your call. (Link: https://calendly.com/traders-lab/success?utm_medium=mail&month=2025-02)

One thing about living abroad vs back in the west you realize how much of a huge scam our internet companies are...

here im paying like $23 for 300/200mps up and down. And this is the slowest room in the house. Its like 600-800mps in other rooms. And phone bill is like $10-12 a month for 5g with similar speeds.

they jusy lobby to keep prices high with trash service idk hope he fixs it soon getting kinda crazy

author: Luke
content:
that’s the way to go bro. It took me months to figure out why I was losing so much money. I finally figured out it was actually 2 big reasons:
1. Changing my sizing every trade, and since I had a small account I would size up every day. For example if I had a 3k account and made 1k, I would size up 1k the next day, which was stupid so it’s good you’re not doing that lol.
2. the other reason was because I was chasing and not taking A+ setups and taking trades for the sake of taking them.

author: DWC2016
content:
Thanks bro yea Im trying to keep my trade size from $750-$1000 for probly the next week and scale up a bit then. And I also had the over trading problem no usally my best days are one trade. 

Last week I took a big loss being an idiot doing something I knew I shouldn't that loss made me dive deep into the lab last weekend for hours looking at my trade data and came up with some really good strategies for risk management and holding winners. and it working suprising well tbh. 

This community has been huge like everything in my development really feels SO GOOD to put in the work and see progress this stuff truly works man. Appreaciate you too man the book you recommended really helped.

author: TCTrader1
content: Same here, getting the confidence still. I traded 2 QQQ Puts with for a total TP of $189. My stupid is I tried to get back in and put back  to end up with $112. for the day. (I need to lose that bad habit)

author: DWC2016
content:
No worries man tbh and yea over trading/fomo is like the #1 that will blow your account and make you take stupid trade. 

I noticed Im most dangerous / open to doing dumb stuff after I feel like I missed a move or could have made more. 

Have to just be comfortable with taking your win for the day and setting up for the next day. 

For me after 1  win caused from overtrading usally its not worth the 3-4 times when I over trade. 

usally  I just lose money and end up pissed off when I trade to much. UNLESS I guess if its a true A+ set up but now I just stop after just 1 nice win.  

Had to feel the pain of the cycle to know. But at least you are aware man 🙏

I can do it now but making sure my confidence in the strategy is good before upping the size. Thanks for you feedback today.

author: Luke
content: yeah that books awesome I’m only halfway through it lol. Overtrading/taking “ok” trades is definitely one of the biggest hindrances to profitability. So far this week is looking like my only green week in a long time, and that is definitely because of patience. I will admit I’ve felt fomo a ton this week but haven’t reacted to it at all. I only took 2 clean trades this week, and this is my second biggest week ever because I had no losers to bring it down. I didn’t trade Monday or today, and honestly it’s hard but the best thing to do. I’ll most likely size up in 2 weeks if next week is good for me. Yeah bro I agree this community is awesome, i’m glad you’re loving the process just as much as me

author: Luke
content: I 100% agree, it’s hard to be patient and take a solid trade
